title: Adult Subscription Validator Using Low-Friction Legacy Processors
narrative: Fraudsters exploit adult content subscriptions ($29.95-$39.95 trial charges) processed through legacy high-risk merchant aggregators with minimal AVS/CVV enforcement. Card is validated via adult site trial (MCC 5967) at 02:00-05:00 UTC when fraud monitoring is lightest, followed 8-14 hours later by high-value electronics purchases ($800-$2,400) from different geographies. Adult merchants historically accept higher declines and rarely challenge chargebacks, making them ideal low-scrutiny validators. Fraudsters use temporary email domains (guerrillamail, tempmail) for subscription signup. Pattern exploits the psychological reluctance of cardholders to dispute adult charges immediately, providing 48-72 hour window before card shutdown. Subscription processor typically shows ProductCD='C' (continuous payment) with addr1 mismatch and dist1 values >500km between validator and exploitation transactions.
indicators: Initial transaction $29.95-$39.95 at MCC 5967 between txn_hour 2-5
P_emaildomain in temporary provider list (guerrillamail, 10minutemail, tempmail)
Follow-on transaction 8-14 hours later with TransactionAmt >$800 and different MCC (5732, 5946, 5411)
addr1 mismatch between subscription and follow-on purchase (null on first, populated on second)
card1_txn_count_24hr increases from 1 to 3+ with card1_amt_zscore jump >2.5
ProductCD changes from 'C' to 'W' or 'H' between validator and exploitation
summary: An adult subscription trial charge ($29.95-$39.95) appears at MCC 5967 during late-night hours (02:00-05:00 UTC) using a temporary email domain, followed 8-14 hours later by high-value purchases ($800+) from a different location over 500km away with mismatched address details.
reasoning: Criminals use adult merchant trials as low-scrutiny card validators because victims hesitate to dispute these charges immediately, buying fraudsters a 48-72 hour window to make expensive purchases before the card is shut down.
indicator_explanations: Small-dollar adult subscription purchase hits during graveyard shift hours
Burner email from a disposable service masks the true cardholder
High-value purchase in unrelated category follows hours after the test charge
Subsequent transaction appears hundreds of miles away from the initial test
Address field left empty on validator then suddenly populated for fraud purchase
Card accelerates from single transaction to multiple with large amount spike
Purchase type switches from digital content to physical goods or wallet transfer
